title: Private Trading and Order Flow
summary: Why confidential infrastructure matters for trading products, from hidden order intent and fairer execution to the product advantage of protecting high-value financial activity.

bodySections.title: Why transparent markets leak too much
bodySections.body: On fully transparent rails, orders reveal intent before the user receives the intended outcome. That creates opportunities for frontrunning, manipulation, and strategic behavior by observers.

bodySections.title: What Arcium changes
bodySections.body: Arcium makes it easier to imagine trading systems where the user gets the benefit of computation without publishing the valuable information that would otherwise be exploited in real time.

bodySections.title: The practical test
bodySections.body: A strong private trading use case should improve execution quality, user trust, or compliance outcomes. If the privacy layer adds complexity without changing those outcomes, the use case is probably weak.
